name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: Cash America Pawn at 15421 1st Ave S, Burien, WA 98148 in the Downtown neighborhood is Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.3 stars across 261 reviews. Call +1 206-243-1696.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn store chain. Founded and developed into a publicly traded company (component of S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indices), FirstCash has built a network exceeding 3,300 retail pawn locations across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin American countries including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. The company employs approximately 22,000 people globally and serves cash-constrained and credit-constrained consumers as its primary market.

FirstCash's core business model centers on pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property—and retail sales. They buy and sell a broad range of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. The company emphasizes immediate cash payment for items of value and offers fast pawn loan processing. Beyond traditional pawn operations, FirstCash provides layaway services (with 10% down payment options) and actively buys gold, silver, and platinum for customers needing immediate liquidity.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its scale and geographic reach. With over 2,500 stores across North and Latin America, they provide extensive accessibility compared to independent pawn shops. The company also operates AFF, a wholly owned subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through over 15,000 active retail merchant partner locations, indicating diversification beyond traditional pawn lending. Their technology infrastructure appears modern, with online store locators and inventory systems.

However, pawn loans remain a high-cost borrowing method. While non-recourse (meaning you don't owe money if the item sells for less), pawn loans typically carry implicit interest rates far exceeding traditional personal loans or credit-based alternatives. Customers must forfeit possession of items during the loan period, making this suitable only for non-essential goods. The retail merchandise sold carries no quality guarantees, and customers should view pawn shops as emergency cash sources rather than primary financial institutions.
pros: Largest international pawn operator with 3,300+ locations ensuring accessibility and standardized service quality
Non-recourse loans mean no debt obligation if pledged item sells for less than loan amount
Fast cash funding on the spot with no credit check required
Diverse merchandise bought (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments) provides flexible collateral options
Layaway service available with only 10% down payment for retail purchases
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) indicating regulatory oversight and financial stability
Buys gold, silver, and platinum for immediate cash needs without requiring loan structures
cons: Pawn loans carry extremely high implicit interest rates compared to personal loans, making them expensive short-term debt
Loss of possession during loan period means customers cannot use pledged items, limiting utility for essential goods
No quality guarantees on retail merchandise purchased from pawn stores
Non-recourse structure benefits lender more than borrower if collateral value fluctuates
Limited loan amounts determined by item valuation rather than borrower's actual financial need
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash needs and non-essential items to pledge as collateral
Unbanked or credit-constrained individuals unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
People seeking quick liquidation of jewelry, electronics, or tools without waiting for private sales
Customers looking for affordable layaway options to purchase merchandise with minimal down payment
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or credit-constrained consumers needing emergency cash who own non-essential items to pledge as collateral. The critical caveat is that pawn loans remain expensive short-term debt with implied interest rates far exceeding personal loans or credit-based alternatives—this should only be used when traditional borrowing is unavailable and the cash need is genuine and immediate.
services: Pawn loans secured by pledged personal property (non-recourse)
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Gold, silver, and platinum buying services with immediate cash payment
Layaway services with 10% down payment option
Item appraisal and valuation for collateral assessment
Store locator and inventory search tools
Fast loan processing without credit checks
Lease-to-own payment solutions through subsidiary AFF (15,000+ merchant partners)
Multi-state and international pawn store network with consistent operations
